WakeTask is an iPhone alarm accountability app. Instead of letting a user dismiss an alarm passively, it guides them through a wake run: dismiss the alarm, complete a wake check, finish a first task, and build a reliability history.

The app is built on SwiftAIBoilerplatePro modules for authentication, payments, storage, networking, localization, settings, and design system. WakeTask-specific product code lives in the `Wake` app shell, localization keys, storage models, tests, and exploratory UI flow.

## Current Release Target

- App name: `WakeTask`
- Bundle id: `com.keen.waketask`
- iOS target: iOS 17+
- Toolchain target: Xcode 26.2+ on macOS 15+
- Signing team in project: `LL4Y637Z4Y`
- Version: `1.0` build `1`

## Product Surface

- Wake dashboard with alarm contracts and weekly consistency.
- Alarm creation flow with strictness and first-task commitment.
- Active wake run with mission progression before final dismissal.
- Local wake run persistence using the Storage package.
- Accessibility labels for alarm creation, mission completion, and dismissal controls.
- English and Spanish localization keys for the Wake surface.

```text
SwiftAIBoilerplatePro.xcodeproj/   Xcode project
SwiftAIBoilerplatePro/             App target and Wake UI
Packages/                         Shared packages reused from SwiftAIBoilerplatePro
SwiftAIBoilerplateProTests/        Unit tests, including WakeTask tests

```

## Local Setup

This machine needs full Xcode, not only Command Line Tools.

```bash
open SwiftAIBoilerplatePro.xcodeproj
```

After Xcode installs, run:

```bash
./scripts/local/doctor.sh
./scripts/local/run-simulator.sh build
```

See `LOCAL_TESTING.md` for simulator, physical iPhone, and TestFlight preparation steps.

The app can run in debug with mock auth and placeholder service config. Production service values should be placed in `Config/Secrets.xcconfig`, which is gitignored.

```bash
cp Config/Secrets.example.xcconfig Config/Secrets.xcconfig
bash scripts/update-config.sh
```

Do not commit `Config/Secrets.xcconfig` or generated real credentials.

## Hosted CI

The main gate is `.github/workflows/ios-quality.yml`.

It runs on GitHub-hosted macOS and checks:

- `xcodebuild` package resolution, build, and tests
- SwiftLint
- SwiftFormat
- Qlty
- sensitive-value scanning
- Release binary App Store 4.3 string audit
- WakeTask exploratory UI traversal
- uploaded `reports/ios/ios-quality-report.json`
- uploaded `reports/ios/appium-exploratory-report.json`

Manual dispatch:

```bash
gh workflow run "iOS Quality" --ref main
```

Watch:

```bash
gh run list --workflow ios-quality.yml --limit 5
gh run watch <run-id>
```

Actual App Store submission should happen only after a successful TestFlight/internal QA pass.

## Flow References

### 1) Alarmy iOS - Alarm Setup + Mission Flow
- Product: Alarmy
- Source: Alarmy iOS Zendesk section and mission articles.
- Flow name: `Set Alarm → Configure Mission → Dismiss Mission`
- Screen types: onboarding (first launch/permissions assumptions), home/alarm list, mission configuration screen, alarm active screen, mission confirmation screen, settings.
- Observed transitions:
  - From home, user taps `+` to add alarm, then opens `Mission`, picks mission type and taps `Done`.
  - Alarm triggers and opens flow via push notification; user moves into Alarmy app to dismiss.
  - Mission executes; success state -> dismiss and stop sequence.
  - Optional Wake up check enabled -> after mission completion, wait timer then popup "Yes, I am up!" and conditional auto-revive if timeout.
- Empty/error states:
  - Mission time limit too short with adjustable `Mission time limit` choices.
  - No mission sensor input reliability: shake mode can fail, fallback to another mission.
  - Mission without unlock: cannot overlay lock screen; requires notification tap and app open.
  - Alarm left on other devices auto-dismisses after 3 hours.
  - Emergency mode unavailable yet; workaround currently is app reinstall.
- what_to_adapt:
  - Keep mission onboarding explicit (mission type choice before launch).
  - Surface mission timeout and confirmation requirements before scheduling.
  - Include a verification step for “still awake” behavior after dismissal to reduce rebound behavior.
  - Capture sensor/mode limitations clearly so user can switch mission type.
- what_not_to_copy:
  - Avoid forcing mission completion only after lock-screen notification without clear fallback.
  - Avoid undisclosed automatic wake/revive behaviors; this should be surfaced to users.
- private_only: true

### 2) Alarmy iOS - Wake Up Check Sub-flow
- Product: Alarmy
- Source: Wake up check support article.
- Flow name: `Alarm Dismiss → Wake up check window → Confirmation`
- Screen types: active_task, completion, home/home-empty state, settings.
- Observed transitions:
  - Enable wake up check during alarm creation.
  - Alarm rings -> user dismisses -> intermediate wait state.
  - Quiet notification appears after wait and app entry lands on popup requiring confirmation.
  - Confirm “Yes, I am up!” -> complete; no response by timer -> alarm revives.
- Empty/error states:
  - Confirm button timeout leads to failure and re-alarm.
  - Premium-only feature gating.
  - Notification has no sound intentionally.
- what_to_adapt:
  - Add explicit post-task verification state with a short timeout and clear failure recovery path.
  - Keep premium/feature gating visible and explicit at setup time.
- what_not_to_copy:
  - Avoid hidden premium gating on flow-critical behaviors.
  - Avoid silent state assumptions that users can miss in background.
- private_only: true

### 3) Alarmy website FAQ - Mission and Premium Positioning
- Product: Alarmy
- Source: https://alar.my/en
- Flow name: `Mission Feature Positioning Flow`
- Screen types: onboarding, home, create_edit, active_task, completion, profile_settings.
- Observed transitions:
  - Promotional flow: install → mission understanding → feature examples (math/photo/shake) → premium feature differentiation (sound power-up, snooze prevention, sleep analysis).
- Empty/error states:
  - Free users can use core; advanced premium workflows not available.
  - No direct flow steps for failure/recovery (positioning only).
- what_to_adapt:
  - Show differentiated feature value in onboarding with concrete mission examples.
  - Align premium messaging to reliability outcomes, not abstract feature bundles.
- what_not_to_copy:
  - Avoid vague “essential vs premium” framing without exact behavioral deltas.
- private_only: true

### 4) Captain Wake - Set Mission → Mission Complete Loop
- Product: Captain Wake
- Source: https://captainwake.io/
- Flow name: `Set Alarm → Escalating Mission -> completion -> insights`
- Screen types: onboarding, home, create_edit, primary_list, active_task, completion, history_streaks.
- Observed transitions:
  - Set alarm and mission in one sequence.
  - On ring, mission required for completion.
  - Mission success leads to completed wake path.
  - Insights tab exposes streak, completion rate, and trends.
- Empty/error states:
  - `Limited snooze` and mission timeout/alternative (`30-second dismiss`), implying intentional escape hatch for edge failure.
- what_to_adapt:
  - Keep mission path short and linear; mission completion should be hard to stall but recoverable.
  - Add low-friction progress stats after completion (streak, completion cadence).
- what_not_to_copy:
  - Avoid too many mission variants without clear progression rules; keep validation objective.
- private_only: true

### 5) Orlo - Alarm + challenge onboarding and paid access
- Product: Orlo
- Source: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/orlo-alarm-wake-up-challenge/id6760335025
- Flow name: `Smart alarm → challenge completion → paywall tier`
- Screen types: onboarding, primary_list, create_edit, active_task, completion, paywall_subscription, history_streaks.
- Observed transitions:
  - Set alarm → choose challenge → complete to stop alarm.
  - Trial start for first challenge use; Pro unlock on subscription.
  - Changelog indicates paywall enforcement tightened (subscription check at launch/nav) and alarm hardening.
- Empty/error states:
  - No explicit user-state recovery details in listing; relies on release notes for enforcement and behavior.
- what_to_adapt:
  - Model onboarding with a short “create challenge, try flow, see value, then gate premium” path.
  - Enforce paywall checks consistently across major navigation points.
- what_not_to_copy:
  - Avoid allowing paywall bypass paths that leave premium features accessible in normal flow.
- private_only: true

### 6) FreakyAlarm - Wake Up then Get Up progression
- Product: FreakyAlarm
- Source: https://www.freakyalarm.com/
- Flow name: `Wake Up -> Get Up -> statistics`
- Screen types: onboarding, home, active_task, completion, history_streaks, profile_settings.
- Observed transitions:
  - Alarm -> mission game challenge -> completion -> statistics screen.
  - Includes mission types and scan/photo/voice variants, plus lockout via button volume and insistent notifications.
- Empty/error states:
  - Notably mission type alternatives imply failure if one method fails. No explicit error copy in public page.
- what_to_adapt:
  - Multi-mode validation is useful for accessibility and hardware variance.
  - Keep statistics as a direct post-completion reinforcement state.
- what_not_to_copy:
  - Avoid overloading challenge set without mapping each path to wake reliability goal.
- private_only: true

### 7) Zingy - Mission-chain + monetization alternatives
- Product: Zingy (launch-prepositioned competitor framing)
- Source: https://zingyalarm.com/
- Flow name: `Mission configuration → mission chains → conversion`
- Screen types: create_edit, active_task, completion, paywall_subscription.
- Observed transitions:
  - Mission choices include chain composition (e.g., Math → Shake → QR).
  - Pricing transition from free baseline to one-time premium or subscription alternatives.
- Empty/error states:
  - FAQ states some mission and billing tradeoffs, including iOS background requirement caveat.
- what_to_adapt:
  - Consider mission chaining (or task step chaining) with clear progress indicator.
  - Show platform limitations (background execution) before relying on it.
- what_not_to_copy:
  - Avoid ambiguous lifetime/susbcription promises if your pricing model differs; be explicit in legal and UX.
- private_only: true

### 8) Todoist - Project/task baseline for list, create/edit, complete, and settings
- Product: Todoist
- Source: https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/get-started-with-todoist-OgNNJR and help subscription settings flow.
- Flow name: `Project onboarding → task create/edit → completion → history/archived`
- Screen types: onboarding, primary_list, create_edit, completion, profile_settings, paywall_subscription.
- Observed transitions:
  - Create project, add task, set due dates from task text, split subtasks, add sections, add to inbox when uncategorized.
  - Navigate to completed tasks via project menu for review of historical completion.
  - Billing flow from settings path: gear icon -> Todoist Pro -> upgrade.
- Empty/error states:
  - Subscription not applied states and refresh path noted in help; migration issues imply state desync cases.
- what_to_adapt:
  - Keep task creation one-tap with minimal required fields and progressive enhancement for labels/dates.
  - Build explicit completion history path (checked/completed log).
  - Offer obvious settings entry points (gear/profile) from persistent list surfaces.
- what_not_to_copy:
  - Avoid burying paid-plan state recovery; make subscription status and plan mismatch handling transparent.
  - Avoid heavy first-run friction before users can complete at least one task.
- private_only: true

### 9) Todoist onboarding examples (flow catalog mirrors)
- Product: Todoist
- Source: Uiland flow collection and Page Flows task flow listing.
- Flow name: `Onboarding and task flow variants`
- Screen types: onboarding, primary_list, create_edit, completion.
- Observed transitions:
  - Multi-step onboarding variants, sign-in entry, tasks/login/paywall variant references.
  - Multi-flow naming suggests standardized progression from initial value proposition to task actions.
- Empty/error states:
  - Full flow screenshots were gated/restricted (some references indicate sign-in required for full view).
- what_to_adapt:
  - Treat onboarding as a variant-driven sequence with clear skip/continue logic.
  - Keep paywall-related flow variants isolated and measurable.
